name: Wretched Aia
bio:
Physical Appearance: Imperial female. Wears only lower-class clothing consisting of a belted vest and a pair of coarse linens. Wanders barefooted through the streets of Bravil, having no shoes to her name - a visible sign of her extreme poverty.
Race: Imperial
Personality Keywords: destitute, desperate, starving, struggling, resilient, suffering
Role: Wretched Aia is a beggar living on the streets of Bravil, surviving day-to-day through begging and, when necessary, stealing food. She represents the extreme poverty and desperation found in Cyrodiil's poorest city. She is one of the "poor and suffering" whom those seeking Speechcraft training from Tandilwe must speak with to demonstrate compassion.
Home: Wretched Aia has no home. Her bedroll is located in the southwestern corner of Bravil, where she sleeps exposed to the elements every night from 10pm to 6am. This is the full extent of her shelter and possessions.
Health: Aia is infected with Collywobbles, a disease that afflicts her constantly. Her illness, combined with her poverty and frequent hunger, makes her existence even more miserable. The disease can spread to others who engage her in combat, though why anyone would fight this desperate, starving woman is unclear.
Daily Routine: Aia wakes at 6am on her bedroll and walks to the northern gate where she begs for four hours until 10am. She then relocates to beg in front of the Lonely Suitor Lodge until 2pm. From 2pm to 6pm she tries her luck at the southern side of the Great Chapel of Mara - an unwise location since no one ever passes through there, ensuring hours of fruitless begging. At 6pm, if she has managed to find any food during her day of begging, she takes it to the bench in front of the Lonely Suitor Lodge to eat. If she hasn't found food, she's forced to steal from Cosmus the Cheat's food sack near his bedroll behind The Fair Deal. If she finds food in his sack, she brings it to the bench to eat; if even Cosmus's sack is empty, she simply drifts around starving until she returns to her bedroll at 10pm.
The Struggle for Food: Aia's daily existence revolves entirely around finding enough food to survive. She spends all day begging at different locations around Bravil, hoping someone will take pity on her. When begging fails - which appears to be often - she's reduced to stealing from another destitute person's meager supplies. The fact that even Cosmus the Cheat's food sack is sometimes empty speaks to the desperate poverty of Bravil's street population. On nights when she finds nothing, she goes to bed hungry, having spent the entire day searching for a single meal that never came.
Personality - The Utterly Destitute: Wretched Aia embodies absolute poverty and desperation. Her very name - "Wretched" - defines her entire existence. She owns nothing but the clothes on her back, not even shoes for her bare feet. She's sick with disease she cannot afford to treat. She spends every waking hour begging, moving from location to location in hopes that a different spot might yield better results. When the Great Chapel proves fruitless (as it always does), she doesn't give up - she moves on to stealing, then drifting hungry through the streets. Despite this unrelenting hardship - homelessness, illness, hunger, barefoot wandering through all weather - she continues. She wakes each morning at 6am and begins the cycle again: beg, relocate, beg, relocate, search for food, sleep on the ground, repeat. There's a grim resilience in this routine, though whether it's hope or simply the instinct to survive is impossible to say. She represents the invisible suffering that exists in every city - those who fall through every crack, whom society has abandoned to starve in the streets while the Great Chapel of Mara stands nearby, apparently offering no charity to those who need it most.
Bravil's Poverty: That Aia spends hours begging at the southern side of the Great Chapel where "no one ever passes through" highlights the indifference and poor planning that characterize her desperate life. She either doesn't know this location is useless or has nowhere else to go. Either way, it ensures her continued hunger and suffering.
author: Bethesda
